baseline_input_weight_path.read: Reads materialized probs and grad_logits_scaled [S, topk, H] tensors from HBM.

constant_weight_diagnostic.read: Fills P and dP shared tiles from a compile-time constant instead of reading materialized probs/grad_logits. This is not semantic sparse-MLA backward; it is a read-elision upper-bound diagnostic.

read: Eliding the two materialized [S, topk, H] weight reads does not move the current TileLang compact producer: constant weights are effectively identical to the real input-weight path at S4096. The current 10.96 ms wall is therefore not mainly HBM reads of probs/grad_logits; it is the MFMA/local active-table/selected-lane producer structure. Fusing LI/probability production may still be required for correctness and memory, but it is not enough by itself to reach the 8.0-8.8 ms sparse-atomic wall. The next speed branch should change producer topology: more parallel segmented/grouped reduction or a CK/rocWMMA schedule that preserves query-major reuse without one CTA carrying a large active table.
